🇮🇷The war in Iran has shut down the Strait of Hormuz, choking off roughly one-fifth of the world’s oil and LNG supply and sending energy prices soaring.

Asia is feeling the biggest pinch as the main destination for Gulf oil, while Europe scrambles to cut demand and Africa braces for higher fuel costs and inflation.

With over 90% of new renewable projects now cheaper than fossil fuels in 2024 (per IRENA), the disruption is pushing many to see renewables as the only real long-term fix for this kind of fragility.

Source: Al Jazeera

🚨🇮🇷🇺🇸 THE MEGA COLLAPSE THE END OF GLOBALIZATION AS WE KNOW IT

The globalized world is fracturing in real time as countries move toward total energy independence to survive the chaos. 

Geopolitical analyst Pepe Escobar reports that a tsunami of capital is fleeing the Gulf for Southeast Asia while European businessmen face a state of absolute despair. 

We are witnessing the beginning of a mega collapse of the global economy that will be felt for decades.

@RealPepeEscobar

When I'm looking at price relative to 200-day average, I'm using it as a trend filter.

My aim is to find out if indices are entering into a lengthy several months correction/downtrend which will then allow me to focus on short chart pattern setups.

I am not using the 200-day breach as a signaling tool.

I.e. is #MIBTEL headed for something like 2022?

🚨IRAN WAR UPDATE: DAY 21

🇮🇷 President Pezeshkian said Iran does not want division with Muslim nations, calling them “our brothers.”

🇮🇷 Iran fired two intermediate-range ballistic missiles at Diego Garcia, the U.S.–UK base ~4,000 km away in the Indian Ocean.

🇮🇱 Missiles streaked over Tel Aviv and the West Bank as Iran launched another barrage toward Israel.

🇺🇸 The Iran war has cost the U.S. over $25B so far, averaging ~$1.3B per day.

🇮🇱 The Israeli Air Force struck dozens of military sites in Tehran and central Iran, including weapons production facilities.

🇱🇧 Israeli airstrikes reportedly destroyed multiple civilian homes in Beirut’s southern suburbs.

🇰🇼 Kuwait’s Mina Al-Ahmadi refinery, the country’s largest at 730,000 barrels/day, took another Iranian drone hit, sparking fires in multiple units for the second time in 24 hours. No injuries reported.

🇺🇸 The Pentagon dismissed claims Iran could close the Strait of Hormuz for six months, calling it an “extreme hypothetical.”

Rogan read a report that US base commanders told troops the Iran war is "God's plan" and part of Armageddon. 

If true, military leaders using scripture to justify a war to their own soldiers is one of the most dangerous and disturbing things imaginable.
https://t.co/2Cgn3OYiV0

🇮🇷 Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei hasn't been seen in public since taking office, sparking widespread speculation he's still badly hurt or sidelined from previous strikes.

Iranian officials are openly saying they'll keep messing with international shipping through the Strait of Hormuz long after the war ends, basically vowing to hold the global energy choke point hostage indefinitely.

The combo of a missing leader and this defiant energy threat keeps the pressure on hard, with no sign of Tehran backing down anytime soon.

Source: Study of War

🇺🇸 The U.S. Navy just unleashed the Liberty Class, a 190-foot crewless cargo beast that can cross 11,500 miles of open ocean carrying 165 tons with zero humans on board.

Expect 10-20 of these autonomous haulers every year. 
https://t.co/WB7ZNYsvP9

🚨🇺🇸🇨🇳THE CHINA CHOKEPOINT: STRATEGIC SABOTAGE IN THE STRAITS

China was counting on Iranian "floating storage" to survive the war, but that safety net is now under threat.

World-renowned energy markets expert Anas Alhajji reveals how the U.S. can use international law to board ships and effectively block China’s energy lifeline.

This isn't just a regional war; it is a calculated effort to use the Strait of Hormuz as a chokepoint against America's biggest rival.

@AnasAlhajji

🚨🇺🇸🇨🇴The DEA has officially named Colombian President Gustavo Petro a “priority target” in multiple drug trafficking investigations dating back to 2022.

The probes include alleged ties to Mexico’s Sinaloa cartel and claims that his representatives solicited bribes from traffickers in exchange for blocking their extradition to the U.S.

Petro strongly denies everything.

Trump is calling bullshit. 

Source: AP

Truly incredible:

The Nasdaq 100 ETF, $QQQ, surged +1.1% between 3:40 PM ET and 5:00 PM ET today without ANY major news.

Options flows on $QQQ long calls were surging into the 4:00 PM ET market close.

At 5:13 PM ET, President Trump said he is considering “winding down” the Iran war.

$QQQ is now up +2% from its low, with March 23rd dated calls set to open +200% above today’s lows.

It’s going to be an eventful weekend.

RT @KobeissiLetter: What just happened?

At 2:30 PM ET today, CBS News reported that President Trump was considering "boots on the ground"…

From the Jan 27 peak the S&P 500 has now pulled back 6.8%.

This comes after a 40% rally off the April lows and a nine month win streak (total return). 

No, it isn't fun, but good to remember most years see a double digit correction at some point (24 of 46 since 1980).

Out of those 24, 14 managed to finish in the green and finished up 17.4% on avg.

We came into this year and said a 12-15% correction was quite likely. Not that it was a crazy call really, given the avg year has a 14.1% correction on average.

$SMH (VanEck Semi ETF) in focus as many parts of Tech have begun to wobble a bit while QQQ just undercut last Nov lows.  @IBDinvestors @marketsurge 

While still in consolidation here, any weekly close under $374 would be problematic & this also lines up with trends from last April.

Since 1950, there have been 30 other times the S&P 500 was above it's 200-day MA for more than 200 days.

Is it a bad thing when these long streaks end? 

Not really, as future returns appear to be about normal. 6 months later? Up 4.9% on avg. 12 months later? Up 8.3% on avg. https://t.co/mxbLjHhipP

What just happened?

At 2:30 PM ET today, CBS News reported that President Trump was considering "boots on the ground" in Iran.

Then, at 3:43 PM ET, President Trump said "I don't want to do a ceasefire with Iran," with the S&P 500 hitting a new 2026 low.

Exactly 90 minutes later, at 5:13 PM ET, President Trump said the US is "considering winding down" the war with Iran.

Between the 3:43 PM ET and 5:13 PM ET comments, the S&P 500 had already risen nearly +1% on NO news.

By 6:15 PM ET, the S&P 500 rallied +1.8% from its low, adding +$900 BILLION in market cap.

Markets are now closed until Monday.

After 214 trading days, the S&P 500 closed beneath it's 200-day MA this week.

Since 1950, when the S&P 500 closes above this trendline the annualized return is 21.1%.

When it closes beneath? -22.2%. 

Proving once again that bad things tend to happen beneath this trendline. https://t.co/HOYMj3i41w

BREAKING: The Trump Administration has made "detailed preparations" for deploying US ground forces into Iran, per CBS News.

Details include:

1. Senior military commanders have sent Trump specific requests aimed at preparing for a ground invasion

2. Trump has been "deliberating" whether to position ground forces in the region

3. The US has held meetings to prepare for how to handle the "possible detention of Iranian soldiers and paramilitary operatives" in a ground invasion

4. The US is preparing to deploy elements of the 82nd Airborne Division into the Middle East region

It's going to be another busy weekend ahead.

🚨 Added CORN to my portfolio

Everyone’s talking about oil. Nobody’s talking about food.

Higher fuel costs hit farming and fertilizer prices are moving (we already hold NTR). Grain exports are getting disrupted. Corn goes into feed, fuel and food production.

Remember what happened in 2022 during the Russia/Ukraine conflict?

The options flow on CORN has been extremely aggressive. Big money buying calls at the 25 strike expiring August 2026.

A third of US corn production goes into ethanol.

Ethanol is blended into every gallon of gas you pump. Regular gas is already 10% ethanol.

When oil gets expensive, demand for ethanol goes up. Corn follows.

Gas is up 27% since Feb 28. Farm groups are now pushing Congress to approve year-round E15 sales, 15% ethanol blended fuel instead of 10%.

Anyway, I like the setup here.

Not a call. Just sharing my positioning.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

For those who don’t already know, I share all my moves here publicly. The market moves fast so my posts are very time-sensitive. Turn on notifications so you don’t miss anything, this is important.
